title: Adenocalymma contractum
intro: Adenocalymma contractum, also known as the "Brazilian cherry," is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Adenocalymma contractum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, and is also used in the production of herbal supplements.

The classification of Adenocalymma contractum is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its unique flowers and growth habit.
